title: DNS leak warning only covers .onion; socks5:// vs socks5h:// distinction underdocumented
description:
validate_proxy_config in cli/src/main.rs warns about DNS leaks only when the relay URL contains '.onion'. For clearnet relay URLs, no warning or explanation is given about the socks5:// vs socks5h:// distinction.

In this implementation, both socks5:// and socks5h:// pass the relay hostname as a domain-name (ATYP=3) to the proxy via tokio-socks — there is no local DNS resolution in either case. This is safe, but it is the opposite of what users familiar with curl/ssh proxy conventions expect (socks5:// conventionally resolves DNS locally; socks5h:// resolves at the proxy).

The risk: a privacy-conscious user who knows the conventional semantics may:
- Believe socks5:// is leaking DNS to their ISP when it is not (false alarm)
- OR believe socks5h:// is providing privacy it actually provides, without realizing the implementation ignores the distinction for non-.onion addresses

The --proxy help text ('socks5h:// recommended for .onion addresses') hints at the distinction but does not explain that tokio-socks does not locally resolve hostnames regardless of scheme.

Either the behavior should match the conventional semantics (socks5:// resolves locally, socks5h:// at proxy), or the documentation should explicitly state that this implementation always sends hostnames to the proxy.

Files: cli/src/main.rs validate_proxy_config (~line 179), cli/src/main.rs --proxy arg help text (~line 44).
close_reason: socks5h:// guidance for .onion is correct user advice; implementation detail that tokio-socks sends ATYP=3 regardless is an impl artifact, not a design promise. No change needed.
